pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage of the horse blog showing highlights and quick access to main sections. Includes a prominent search bar and footer newsletter form.
primary_functions: Display latest and featured posts from Training, Health, Competitions, and Gear categories.
Provide quick navigation to key categories, Reading list, Favorites, Gallery, and Contact.
Provide a prominent site-wide search bar to start article searches (e.g., for 'oats feed').
Provide access to the global footer, which includes the newsletter subscription form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Header navigation: click 'Training', 'Health', 'Competitions', or 'Gear' or click a category quick link card.
Click a featured or latest article title/card.

params.query: searchTerm entered in homepage search field
params.category: optional category filter from search UI

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the homepage search form (e.g., query 'oats feed').
Click 'Reading list' in the header.
Click 'Favorites' in the header.
Click 'Photos' in the header or a featured gallery teaser.
Click 'Contact' in the header or a contact CTA in content.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Home' item in the main navigation.
Primary landing URL of the site (index.html).

pages.name: Article List
pages.description: Category listing page used for Training, Health, Competitions, and Gear articles with filters and sorting. Used heavily for training, health, and competition browsing tasks.
primary_functions: Show a list of posts for a selected category via URL parameter (e.g., categoryId=training, health, competitions, gear).
Provide tag filters (e.g., Jumping, Lameness, Tricks) and year/archive filters (e.g., 2024, 2023) to narrow posts.
Provide sort options such as 'Newest first' and 'Oldest first' to reorder posts within the current filters.
Allow saving posts directly from the list to the Reading list via a 'Save' or bookmark icon without opening the post.
Allow favoriting posts directly from the list (where needed) and navigating into any post via its title to the Article Detail page.

incoming_params.description: Required article category to list: 'training', 'health', 'competitions', or 'gear'.
Optional tag filter (e.g., tag for 'Jumping', 'Lameness', 'Tricks'). Mapped to interface parameter filterTagId.
Optional sort order key, e.g. 'newest_first' or 'oldest_first'.
Current pagination page number for the article list.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the title of an article in the list.
Click the 'Reading list' link/icon in the header.
Click the 'Favorites' link/icon in the header.

params.tagId: id of selected tag from tag filter

outgoing_connections.trigger: Change a tag filter, year filter, sort option, or pagination on the category list.
Click 'Home' in the header.
Click 'Photos' in the header.
Click 'Contact' in the header.

params.query: search term entered into the header search bar

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the header search form from this page.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Training', 'Health', 'Competitions', or 'Gear' header links with categoryId parameter.
Accessed when following category or tag breadcrumbs that specify categoryId, tagId, year, and sortOrder.

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single article (Training, Health, Competitions, Gear) with full content, comments, saving/favoriting actions, and contextual poll support.
primary_functions: Display full article content, including title, body, tags, published date, and breadcrumbs back to its category and tag (e.g., Training > Jumping).
Allow saving or removing the article from the Reading list via a 'Save to reading list' or bookmark button.
Allow adding or removing the article from Favorites via an 'Add to favorites' or 'Save' button near the title.
Provide a share area with actions such as 'Copy link' to copy the URL of the current article.
Display a comment section showing existing comments and a form with Name, Email, and Comment fields to submit new comments (e.g., for lameness and tricks posts).
Host contextual interactive elements such as the 'Which trick should Star learn next?' poll in the sidebar, including selecting an option (e.g., 'Bow') and submitting a vote.

incoming_params.description: ID of the article whose full detail should be displayed.

params.tagId: optional primary tag from this article (for breadcrumb tag link)
params.year: year component of article's published_at, when using archive link

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a category or tag breadcrumb (e.g., 'Training' or 'Jumping') or archive link.
Click 'Reading list' in the header to review saved posts.
Click 'Favorites' in the header to review favorited posts and photos.
Click a 'Contact about this article' link (if present) in the article meta area.
Click 'Home' in the header or breadcrumb home icon.
Click 'Photos' in the header.

params.query: search term entered into the header search bar

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the header search form from this page.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessed by navigating to article_detail.html with an articleId parameter from lists, search results, favorites, or reading list.
Shared URLs (e.g., via 'Copy link') can be opened directly in the browser.

pages.name: Reading List
pages.description: Page listing all articles the user has saved to their personal reading list, with options to group by category for reading plans.
primary_functions: Display all posts saved to the Reading list from either the Article List or Article Detail pages.
Allow opening any saved article by clicking its title to return to the Article Detail page.
Provide options to remove individual posts from the Reading list.
Offer sorting and grouping controls, including grouping by 'Category', so a 5-post reading plan can be organized into Training, Health, and Gear sections.

incoming_params.description: Optional grouping mode, e.g., 'none' or 'category', mapped to getReadingListItems.groupBy.
Optional sort key for saved items (e.g., 'added_at_desc').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the title of a saved article in the reading list.
Change the grouping or sorting controls on the reading list page.
Click a category label for a saved article to see more posts in that category.
Click 'Home' in the header.
Click 'Favorites' in the header.
Click 'Photos' in the header.
Click 'Contact' in the header.

params.query: term entered in the header search bar

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the header search form from the reading list page.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Reading list' link in the main navigation.
Grouping and sorting options are reflected in groupBy and sortBy URL parameters.

pages.name: Favorites
pages.description: Unified favorites page showing favorited posts (stories) and favorited photos, including a simple photo viewer for navigating between saved images.
primary_functions: Display a list of all favorited articles (e.g., earliest 2023 competition story) with titles linking back to their Article Detail pages.
Display a grid or list of all favorited photos (e.g., selected 2022 competitions gallery images) as thumbnails.
Allow opening a favorited article by clicking its title to revisit it from Favorites.
Allow opening a favorited photo in a larger viewer (lightbox-style) from its thumbnail and navigate between favorite photos using next/previous controls.

incoming_params.description: Optional filter for which favorites section to emphasize, such as 'all', 'articles', or 'photos'.
Optional ID of a favorited photo to show as the initially opened image in the large viewer.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the title of a favorited article.
Click a favorited photo thumbnail to open it in the large viewer (photoId reflected in URL).
Click 'Home' in the header.
Click a category label on a favorited article card.
Click 'Photos' in the header or a 'Browse all photos' link.
Click 'Contact' in the header.
Submit the header search form from the favorites page.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Favorites' link in the main navigation.
The optional view and photoId parameters control which section is focused and which photo is open.

pages.name: Photo Gallery
pages.description: Gallery page for browsing photos of the horse with filters for year and subject (e.g., competitions), and the ability to favorite specific images.
primary_functions: Show image thumbnails with filters such as year (e.g., 2022) and category tabs (e.g., Competitions) to narrow which photos are displayed.
Allow the user to scroll through the gallery and mark specific photos as favorites via a heart or 'Favorite' icon on each thumbnail.
Provide navigation to the Favorites page (e.g., a 'My favorites' link) where only favorited photos are shown.
Allow clicking on thumbnails (optionally) to open a larger view within the page or overlay for closer inspection.

incoming_params.description: Current pagination page number for gallery photos.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Change the year filter or category tab at the top of the gallery.
Change gallery pagination.
Click 'Favorites' in the header or a 'My favorites' link in the gallery.
Click 'Home' in the header.
Click a contextual link from the gallery to related Training or Competitions stories.
Click 'Contact' in the header.
Submit the header search form from the gallery page.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Photos' link in the main navigation.
Year and category filters are represented via year and category URL parameters.

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact page for sending questions or feedback (including nutrition questions) to the blog owner, with inquiry type selection.
primary_functions: Provide a contact form with fields for Name, Email, Subject, and Message so the user can write detailed inquiries.
Include a dropdown or selector for inquiry type (e.g., 'Nutrition advice') to categorize the message.
Allow the user to reference specific articles and their exact published dates within the message (e.g., questions about oats feeding).
Submit the completed form via a 'Send message' or 'Submit' button and display a confirmation state on the same page.

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of an article that the contact message relates to, used to pre-associate the inquiry on submit.
Optional subject line prefill for the contact form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Home' in the header after sending a message.
Use the header navigation to go to a content category from the contact page.
Click 'Photos' in the header.
Click 'Reading list' in the header.
Click 'Favorites' in the header.
Submit the header search form from the contact page.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact' link in the main navigation.
May be linked from article pages as 'Contact about this article' with a relatedArticleId parameter.

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Page displaying search results for queries entered in the site-wide search bar, including from the homepage and header.
primary_functions: Read the current search term from the query parameter (e.g., query=oats_feed) and display matching article results.
Show a list of result snippets with titles, categories, and short excerpts for each matching article.
Allow the user to click any result title to open the corresponding article on the Article Detail page.
Provide controls (or a visible search box) to refine and resubmit searches without needing to return to the homepage.

incoming_params.description: The free-text search term entered by the user (e.g., 'oats feed').
Optional category filter for search results (e.g., 'health').
Number of search results per page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a search result title to open the full article.

params.query: refined query entered into the search box on this page

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the search form on the Search Results page.
Click a pagination control to move between result pages.
Click 'Home' in the header.
Click a category filter link in the search results sidebar or header.
Click 'Photos' in the header.
Click 'Reading list' in the header.
Click 'Favorites' in the header.
Click 'Contact' in the header.
Click 'About' in the footer.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Search' footer link (with an empty or prefilled query).
Accessed whenever a search is submitted from any page, passing query (and optionally category, page, and pageSize) in the URL.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Informational page about the horse, the author, and the purpose of the blog. Content is loaded via an interface and includes links back to key sections.
primary_functions: Explain the background of the horse, riding focus, and blog goals to give context to Training, Health, and Competition posts.
Provide secondary navigation back to main sections and reiterate how to get in touch (linking to the Contact page).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to blog' or 'Home' link in the About content.
Click contextual links in the About text to view Training, Health, and Competition posts.
Click a 'Contact the author' link in the About page.
Click a 'See Star in photos' link or the 'Photos' header link.
Click 'Reading list' in the header.
Click 'Favorites' in the header.
Submit the header search form from the About page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' link in the site footer.
Can be reached directly via about.html.

header_links.description: Blog homepage with recent and featured posts
Training articles, including Jumping and Tricks posts
Health and care articles, including lameness topics
Gear and equipment reviews and tips
Photo gallery of the horse with filters and favorites
List of articles saved to read later
Favorited posts and photos collected in one place
Contact form for questions and feedback

footer_links.description: Information about the horse and the blog author
Contact page for all inquiries, including nutrition advice
Search results page reached after entering a query
Manage and view saved articles for future reading
View and manage all favorited posts and photos
